summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates selling covered calls on Broadcom to generate premium income (~$620 per two-day cycle) to fund a vacation trip, targeting five cycles to cover the cost. He buys 100 shares at $40,654 and repeatedly sells out-of-the-money calls at the 407.50 strike, exploiting Broadcom's biweekly expiration cycle.
takeaways: Sell covered calls at the next price right out of the money (DITM by one strike)
Broadcom offers covered calls expiring every other day enabling rapid premium cycling
Each covered call contract on 100 shares generates ~$620 premium at current levels
Sell at the bid column (enter negative contract quantity)
Use Fibonacci retracements to identify support levels for optimal entry (e.g., 360 support on Broadcom)
Five cycles of $620 premium = $3,100 total trip funding target
Monitor market technicals; expect pullbacks after all-time highs in account or broader indices

strategy_candidates.change: Integrate bi-weekly or daily expiration cycles (like Broadcom's every-other-day structure) into Premium Desk rotation to accelerate premium collection toward $1,250/mo target
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates that Broadcom's frequent expirations enable $620 per two-day cycle; scaling this across multiple tickers with similar structures would compress timeframe to hit monthly premium goals faster than standard weekly/monthly wheels

title: Covered calls pay for my vacation
